title: About NIH | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
content: About NIH The National Institutes of Health (NIH), a part of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, is the nation’s medical research agency—making important discoveries that improve health and save lives. Who We Are Image NIH is made up of 27 Institutes and Centers, each with a specific research agenda, often focusing on particular diseases or body systems. The NIH headquarters, known as the “NIH campus,” are located in Bethesda, Maryland. The NIH Director NIH Leadership Organization Voices of the NIH Community History What We Do Image NIH’s mission is to seek fundamental knowledge about the nature and behavior of living systems and the application of that knowledge to enhance health, lengthen life, and reduce illness and disability. Mission and Goals Budget The NIH Almanac NIH...Turning Discovery Into Health® Impact of NIH Research Science, Health, and Public Trust Virtual Tour of the NIH Campus Explore the Bethesda campus and how NIH turns discovery into health. Visitor Information Directions, maps, parking information, and more. Frequently Asked Questions Answers to frequently asked questions about the NIH. Contact Us How to contact NIH by email, phone, or mail. Get Involved at NIH Image NIH encourages and depends on public involvement in federally supported research and activities. Get involved Jobs at NIH Image We’re looking for driven and inspired people to join our team. Discover your future This page last reviewed on April 14, 2026
